agent.runner_summary: The simulator builds one WorldCupAgent with a recording FootballTools instance, then calls its async answer(message, session_id) method with plain message-string turns by default.

components.when_to_use: Use for isolated prediction regressions where the matchup is known and only the probability payload or explanation factors are in question.
components.input_guidance: Build FixedComponentInput with kwargs {"team_a": "Brazil", "team_b": "Germany"} using the original team names from the log.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate team_a_win, draw, team_b_win, confidence, and top_factors; ignore data_freshness unless the test explicitly mocks time.

components.when_to_use: Use when a response references the wrong team attributes or the agent should have surfaced profile details for a named team.
components.input_guidance: Build FixedComponentInput with kwargs {"team_name": "France"} or the exact team mentioned in the conversation.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned profile fields such as name, elo, fifa_rank, host, and availability counts.

components.when_to_use: Use for schedule filtering bugs or stale fixture handling without running the full chat flow.
components.input_guidance: Build FixedComponentInput with kwargs {"team_name": "USA"} for filtered schedule cases or {"team_name": null} for the full loaded slate.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned fixtures list, especially team filtering, date fields, and source-dependent schedule content.

components.when_to_use: Use for freshness messaging regressions or cache-age handling without invoking the whole agent.
components.input_guidance: Build FixedComponentInput with kwargs {} because this component takes no arguments.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate exists, stale, source, synced_at, age_seconds, and match_count.

components.when_to_use: Use for isolated standings regressions when the issue is the returned table rather than the full chat response.
components.input_guidance: Build FixedComponentInput with kwargs {"group": "A"} for one group or {"group": null} for the full placeholder table.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate group, team, played, points, and goal_difference values in the returned standings rows.

log_notes: Agent turns should usually use the raw chat message string, for example /predict Brazil vs Germany.
The adapter remains backward-compatible with object turns containing message, optional session_id, and optional channel fields when a learning environment needs them.
Transcript tool_call and tool_result events come from the same FootballTools methods used by both the fallback path and the optional OpenAI Agents SDK tool closures.
Transcript agent_message events include metadata fields for mode, data, data_source, execution_mode, live_openai_requested, live_openai_used, sdk_agent_available, and session_id.
When RELAI_SIMULATOR_ENABLE_LIVE_OPENAI is not set to 1, the adapter forces the deterministic fallback path even if OPENAI_API_KEY is present.
learning_env_guidance: Use FixedInput with plain message-string turns for most regressions in the chat flow.
Use object turns only when a test specifically needs session_id or channel metadata and the installed RELAI SDK supports non-string FixedTurn content.
Use FixedInput only when the exact user wording or a short scripted sequence is important to the failure.
Use PersonaInput when user persistence, tone, or ambiguity matters more than exact phrasing.
Use LLMJudgeEvaluator for semantic answer quality and CodeEvaluator for deterministic checks over transcript metadata or component outputs.
Prefer the component targets for isolated prediction, schedule, freshness, standings, or profile regressions instead of end-to-end agent runs.
